description: Commentary lenses that turn ranked clips into transformative educational content. Each angle maps to taxonomy.json subcategory ids and carries a clip-selection rule: if a clip doesn't satisfy the rule, it doesn't go in a Short built on that angle.

interrogation_tactics.label: Interrogation Tactics, Decoded
interrogation_tactics.teaches: The named techniques detectives actually use — minimization, maximization, theme development, evidence reveals, strategic silence — and why they work (and when they produce false confessions).
interrogation_tactics.selection_rule: Only include a clip if a nameable technique is visible ON camera and visibly changes the suspect's behavior in the clip.
interrogation_tactics.script_formula: Name the technique ('This is minimization') → show the 8-second moment it lands → explain the psychology + the one caveat (e.g., why this technique is banned for minors in some states).

deception_analysis.label: The Science of Lies
deception_analysis.teaches: Statement analysis: distancing language, tense shifts, overly specific denials, performed grief — presented with the honest caveat that behavior analysis is probabilistic, not proof.
deception_analysis.selection_rule: Only include a clip if there is a specific verbal or behavioral marker you can freeze-frame, caption, and name.
deception_analysis.script_formula: Play the line → freeze + caption the marker ('listen to the tense shift: "she WAS a good wife"') → explain what investigators actually do with this signal.

forensics_explained.label: How They Actually Caught Him
forensics_explained.teaches: The science behind the catch: genetic genealogy trees, touch DNA, fingerprint minutiae, decomposition timelines — real methods, correctly described.
forensics_explained.selection_rule: Only include a case if the forensic method is documented in the public case record — never guess the science.
forensics_explained.script_formula: The impossible-sounding fact ('a smoothie cup solved a 41-year-old murder') → 15-second method explainer with a simple diagram → the arrest moment as payoff.

digital_footprint.label: Your Phone Is a Witness
digital_footprint.teaches: How search history, cell-tower pings, Ring/Fitbit/Alexa data, and metadata become courtroom evidence — digital literacy through true crime.
digital_footprint.selection_rule: Only include a case if the specific data type (searches, pings, device logs) is identifiable and its legal path to court is explainable.
digital_footprint.script_formula: Read the search query out loud → explain how police legally obtained it (warrant to Google, geofence) → what the jury did with it.

legal_literacy.label: Know Your Rights (For Real)
legal_literacy.teaches: Miranda myths, how to actually invoke a lawyer, detained-vs-arrested, search & seizure (plain view, probable cause, exigent circumstances), and what sovereign-citizen arguments get legally wrong.
legal_literacy.selection_rule: Only include a clip if a real legal rule is invoked, waived, or misunderstood on camera — and you can cite the actual doctrine.
legal_literacy.script_formula: The on-camera claim ('you can't search my car!') → the actual rule in one sentence (plain-view doctrine / probable cause) → how it played out because of the rule.

police_procedure.label: Why Cops Do It That Way
police_procedure.teaches: The physics of a PIT maneuver, pursuit-termination policy, felony-stop choreography, K9 deployment rules, taser guidelines, the use-of-force continuum.
police_procedure.selection_rule: Only include a clip if the visible action maps to a written policy or physical principle you can state.
police_procedure.script_formula: Freeze before the action ('watch the front wheel') → the principle in one line (rear-quarter contact breaks traction) → replay at speed with the outcome.

dispatch_protocol.label: What the Dispatcher Heard
dispatch_protocol.teaches: Dispatcher training: what they listen for in background audio, how coded calls are recognized, the questioning protocols that keep callers alive.
dispatch_protocol.selection_rule: Only include a call if the dispatcher's specific action (or the caller's technique) changed the outcome.
dispatch_protocol.script_formula: Play the odd-sounding moment → explain what the dispatcher recognized ('pizza order = can't speak freely') → the rescue as payoff.

criminal_psychology.label: Why They Did It
criminal_psychology.teaches: Escalation patterns, offender typologies, victim selection, what the insanity defense actually requires — criminology, not mind-reading.
criminal_psychology.selection_rule: Only include a case if the pattern generalizes — the clip illustrates a documented criminological concept, not just one person's evil.
criminal_psychology.script_formula: The pattern claim ('family annihilators almost always show this first') → the case as evidence → the research finding in one line.

the_one_mistake.label: The Mistake That Ended It
the_one_mistake.teaches: Evidence-chain reconstruction: how one decision — a kept receipt, a reused phone, a returned-to scene — causally led to the arrest.
the_one_mistake.selection_rule: Only include a case if the causal chain from mistake to arrest is documented — no 'probably this got him caught'.
the_one_mistake.script_formula: State the mistake cold ('he kept the receipt') → 3-step chain: mistake → discovery → arrest → close on the interrogation/arrest face.

courtroom_explained.label: What Just Happened in Court
courtroom_explained.teaches: Sentencing math (consecutive vs concurrent), contempt powers, why plea deals exist, the legal role of victim impact statements, courtroom security procedure.
courtroom_explained.selection_rule: Only include a clip if there's a legal mechanism to explain — the emotion is the hook, the mechanism is the content.
courtroom_explained.script_formula: The dramatic moment → 'here's what legally just happened' (judge added contempt = X days per outburst) → the number that makes it concrete.

under_pressure.label: The Brain in Crisis
under_pressure.teaches: Acute stress response: why suspects run when surrounded, auditory exclusion in shootouts, panic decision-making, why heroes stay calm — with real psychology terms.
under_pressure.selection_rule: Only include a clip if the behavior illustrates a nameable stress response you can define in one sentence.
under_pressure.script_formula: The irrational-looking act ('why run when 6 cops surround you?') → the mechanism (amygdala hijack in one line) → what training does to override it.

system_accountability.label: When the System Polices Itself
system_accountability.teaches: How corrupt officers actually get caught (their own bodycams, FBI audits, internal affairs), why impersonators are prosecuted hard, what bodycam policy changed.
system_accountability.selection_rule: Only include a case if an accountability mechanism — not just an individual's downfall — is the explainable center.
system_accountability.script_formula: The irony hook ('his own bodycam convicted him') → the mechanism (who reviews footage, when) → the consequence with the number (sentence/settlement).

safety_playbook.label: What This Teaches You
safety_playbook.teaches: Directly usable safety knowledge: the pizza-order technique, teaching kids their address for 911, why 0.08 exists, when police can enter for a welfare check.
safety_playbook.selection_rule: Only include a clip if there's an actionable takeaway a viewer could personally use within the Short's last 5 seconds.
safety_playbook.script_formula: The save → why it worked, as instruction ('she kept the call in pizza terms — dispatchers are trained for this') → the direct 'if you ever need this' line.

history_firsts.label: The First Time Ever
history_firsts.teaches: Turning points: the first DNA conviction (Pitchfork 1986), the birth of genetic genealogy (GSK 2018), the first bodycam programs, historic 911 recordings.
history_firsts.selection_rule: Only include a case if the 'first' or turning point is verifiable — history claims get fact-checked hardest.
history_firsts.script_formula: 'Before 1986, this crime was unsolvable' → the breakthrough case → what it changed (every DNA catch since traces to this).
